description: End-to-End Testing Framework - tmux-based PTY emulation for testing goomacs with real terminal interaction

userStories.title: tmux test harness core
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Go test helper that manages a tmux session so that I can write E2E tests that send keys and capture screen output.
acceptanceCriteria: Create e2e/harness.go with a Harness struct containing fields: session (string), binary (string), width/height (int)
Start(t *testing.T, binary string, args ...string) creates a detached tmux session with 'tmux new-session -d -s <session> -x 80 -y 24 <binary> <args>'
Session name is unique per test using t.Name() + random suffix to allow parallel execution
SendKeys(keys string) calls 'tmux send-keys -t <session> <keys>' via exec.Command
Capture() []string calls 'tmux capture-pane -t <session> -p' and returns screen as slice of strings (one per row)
CapturePane() string returns full screen as single string (rows joined by newlines)
Close() kills the tmux session with 'tmux kill-session -t <session>' — registered via t.Cleanup so it runs even on test failure
WaitForContent(substr string, timeout time.Duration) error polls Capture() every 100ms until substr appears or timeout expires
go build ./e2e/... succeeds
go vet ./e2e/... passes
userStories.notes: This is the foundation. Create the e2e/ directory and harness.go. Use os/exec to run tmux commands. The Harness should take *testing.T so it can call t.Helper(), t.Cleanup(), and t.Fatal(). Use a 200ms sleep after Start() to let goomacs initialize before sending keys.

userStories.title: Assertion helpers and golden file support
userStories.description: As a developer, I want assertion helpers for screen content so that I can write readable E2E tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Create e2e/assertions.go with methods on *Harness
AssertLineContains(t, row int, substr string) captures screen and checks row contains substr, fails with 'line %d: expected to contain %q, got %q'
AssertLineEquals(t, row int, expected string) checks exact match with trailing whitespace trimmed
AssertStatusBar(t, substr string) checks second-to-last row (row height-2) contains substr
AssertMessageLine(t, substr string) checks last row (row height-1) contains substr
AssertScreenContains(t, substr string) checks substr appears anywhere in full screen capture
AssertScreenSnapshot(t, name string) compares full screen against e2e/testdata/<name>.golden; writes golden file if UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 env var is set or file doesn't exist
AssertCursorAt(t, row, col int) verifies cursor position using 'tmux display-message -t <session> -p #{cursor_x} #{cursor_y}'
Create e2e/testdata/ directory
go build ./e2e/... succeeds
go vet ./e2e/... passes
userStories.notes: All assertion methods should call t.Helper() for correct line reporting. AssertScreenSnapshot should use os.ReadFile/os.WriteFile for golden files. Row indices are 0-based. The Harness knows its height (24) so status bar is at row 22 and message line is at row 23.

userStories.title: Test infrastructure and TestMain
userStories.description: As a developer, I want test infrastructure that builds the binary once and skips tests when tmux is unavailable.
acceptanceCriteria: Create e2e/e2e_test.go with TestMain that builds goomacs binary via 'go build -o <tmpdir>/goomacs .' and stores path in package-level var
TestMain checks for tmux in PATH using exec.LookPath; if not found, prints 'skipping e2e tests: tmux not found' and exits with 0
TestMain creates a temp directory for the binary and cleans it up after tests
Provide a helper function newHarness(t *testing.T, args ...string) *Harness that creates a Harness with the built binary path
Provide a helper function createTestFile(t *testing.T, name string, content string) string that writes content to t.TempDir()/name and returns the path
go test ./e2e/ -v -run TestSmokeTest passes (add a minimal TestSmokeTest that starts goomacs, waits for *scratch* in status bar, then quits with C-x C-c)
go vet ./e2e/... passes
userStories.notes: The binary path must be a package-level var so all tests can access it. TestMain uses os.Exit(m.Run()) pattern. The smoke test validates the entire harness pipeline works end-to-end. Build the binary from the repo root (parent of e2e/), so use 'go build -o <path> ..' or set the working directory.

userStories.title: Basic editing E2E tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E tests covering text insertion, deletion, and file save.
acceptanceCriteria: Create e2e/basic_test.go with test functions in TestBasicEditing group
Test scratch buffer: open with no args, WaitForContent '*scratch*', AssertStatusBar with '*scratch*'
Test text insertion: type 'Hello, World!', WaitForContent 'Hello, World!', AssertLineContains(t, 0, 'Hello, World!')
Test backspace: type 'abc', SendKeys 'BSpace', WaitForContent 'ab', verify line 0 does not contain 'abc'
Test C-d delete: type 'abc', SendKeys 'C-a C-d', WaitForContent 'bc', AssertLineContains(t, 0, 'bc')
Test newline: type 'line1', SendKeys 'Enter', type 'line2', WaitForContent 'line2', AssertLineContains(t, 0, 'line1'), AssertLineContains(t, 1, 'line2')
Test save: create temp file, open it, type text, SendKeys 'C-x C-s', verify file content on disk with os.ReadFile
Test quit: SendKeys 'C-x C-c', verify tmux session no longer exists (tmux has-session returns error)
All tests pass with 'go test ./e2e/ -v -run TestBasicEditing'
go vet ./e2e/... passes
userStories.notes: For the save test, open goomacs with a temp file path, type some text, save with C-x C-s, then read the file from disk. For quit test, after C-x C-c send a small sleep then check if session exists. Use subtests (t.Run) for organization.

userStories.title: Navigation E2E tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E tests covering cursor movement and scrolling.
acceptanceCriteria: Create e2e/navigation_test.go with test functions in TestNavigation group
Test C-f/C-b: type 'abc', verify cursor at col 3, SendKeys 'C-b', AssertCursorAt col 2, SendKeys 'C-f', AssertCursorAt col 3
Test C-n/C-p: open file with multiple lines, C-n moves cursor down (verify cursor row increments), C-p moves back up
Test C-a/C-e: type text on a line, C-e goes to end (verify cursor col), C-a goes to beginning (verify cursor col 0)
Test C-v/M-v: open file with 50+ lines, C-v scrolls down (verify different content visible), M-v scrolls back up
Test M-</M->: open file with many lines, 'Escape >' jumps to end, 'Escape <' jumps to beginning
Test C-l goto-line: open file with 20+ lines, SendKeys 'C-l', type '10', Enter, verify status bar shows line 10 and cursor at row 9
All tests pass with 'go test ./e2e/ -v -run TestNavigation'
go vet ./e2e/... passes
userStories.notes: For M-v, send 'Escape' then 'v' as separate SendKeys calls (or as 'Escape v'). For M-< and M->, use 'Escape <' and 'Escape >'. Create test files with known content using createTestFile helper. For scroll tests, generate a file with numbered lines like 'Line 1', 'Line 2', etc.

userStories.title: Search E2E tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E tests for incremental search.
acceptanceCriteria: Create e2e/search_test.go with test functions in TestSearch group
Test forward search: open file with known content, C-s then type query, AssertMessageLine contains 'I-search: <query>', verify cursor moved to match
Test search next: C-s C-s advances to next match (verify cursor position changed)
Test backward search: C-r then type query, AssertMessageLine contains 'I-search backward: <query>'
Test search accept: Enter exits search, cursor stays at match, message line clears
Test search cancel: C-g cancels search, cursor returns to original position
Test search backspace: type query, BSpace removes last char, message line shows shorter query
All tests pass with 'go test ./e2e/ -v -run TestSearch'
go vet ./e2e/... passes
userStories.notes: Create test files with repeated words at known positions for predictable search results. Use WaitForContent to wait for the I-search message before asserting cursor position. Remember that search is incremental — each keystroke triggers a search.

userStories.title: Kill, yank, and undo E2E tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E tests for kill/yank and undo operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Create e2e/killyank_test.go with test functions in TestKillYankUndo group
Test C-k kill line: type 'hello world', C-a, C-k, verify line is empty
Test C-y yank: kill text with C-k, move to new line, C-y, verify killed text appears
Test C-w kill region: type text, C-a, C-SPC (C-Space), C-f C-f C-f, C-w, verify first 3 chars removed
Test M-w copy region: set mark, move, 'Escape w', move elsewhere, C-y, verify text pasted without original being deleted
Test C-_ undo: type 'abc', C-_ (C-Minus or C-/), verify last edit reversed
Test consecutive kill accumulation: type two lines, go to first line, C-k C-k (kill line + newline), move down, C-y, verify both lines yanked together
All tests pass with 'go test ./e2e/ -v -run TestKillYankUndo'
go vet ./e2e/... passes
userStories.notes: For C-SPC, send 'C-Space' to tmux. For C-_, send 'C-_'. For M-w, send 'Escape w'. The kill ring accumulation test is important — it verifies that consecutive C-k calls without intervening non-kill commands join the killed text.

userStories.title: Buffer management E2E tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E tests for multi-buffer operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Create e2e/buffer_test.go with test functions in TestBufferManagement group
Test C-x C-f open file: create temp file with known content, C-x C-f type path Enter, WaitForContent with file content, AssertStatusBar with filename
Test C-x b switch buffer: open two files, C-x b type buffer name Enter, verify status bar shows switched buffer
Test C-x C-b buffer list: open multiple files, C-x C-b, verify screen shows all buffer names
Test C-x k kill buffer: open a file, C-x k Enter, verify buffer removed (status bar shows different buffer)
Test tab completion: create temp dir with files, C-x C-f type partial name Tab, verify minibuffer input completed
All tests pass with 'go test ./e2e/ -v -run TestBufferManagement'
go vet ./e2e/... passes
userStories.notes: For file operations, use createTestFile to create temp files with known content. For tab completion test, create a temp directory with uniquely-named files so the completion is deterministic. Remember C-x C-f is two separate SendKeys: 'C-x' then 'C-f'.

userStories.title: Window splitting E2E tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E tests for window splitting operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Create e2e/window_test.go with test functions in TestWindowSplitting group
Test C-x 2 vertical split: SendKeys 'C-x 2', verify two status bars visible (scan rows for '==' pattern appearing twice)
Test C-x 3 horizontal split: SendKeys 'C-x 3', verify vertical separator '│' character appears in capture
Test C-x o switch window: split, C-x o, verify active window indicator changes (== vs -- markers)
Test C-x 0 close window: split, C-x 0, verify only one status bar remains
Test C-x 1 close other windows: split into multiple, C-x 1, verify only one status bar remains
All tests pass with 'go test ./e2e/ -v -run TestWindowSplitting'
go vet ./e2e/... passes
userStories.notes: For vertical split (C-x 2), count rows that look like status bars (contain '==' or '--'). For horizontal split (C-x 3), check for the Unicode box-drawing character '│' (U+2502). After C-x o, the active/inactive markers swap.

userStories.title: Syntax highlighting smoke test
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a basic E2E test that verifies syntax highlighting is active for .go files.
acceptanceCriteria: Create e2e/highlight_test.go with test functions in TestSyntaxHighlighting group

Test .go file highlighting: create temp .go file with 'package main\nfunc main() {}', open it, capture pane with ANSI escapes using 'tmux capture-pane -e -t <session> -p', verify output contains ANSI escape sequences (\033[ or \x1b[)

Test .txt file no highlighting: create temp .txt file with plain text, open it, capture with -e flag, verify NO ANSI escape sequences in the content area (status bar will have escapes from reverse video, so only check content rows)
Add CaptureWithEscapes() method to Harness that calls 'tmux capture-pane -e -t <session> -p' for ANSI-mode capture
All tests pass with 'go test ./e2e/ -v -run TestSyntaxHighlighting'
go vet ./e2e/... passes
userStories.notes: The key insight is that tmux capture-pane normally strips ANSI escapes, but with -e flag it preserves them. A .go file should have color escapes from Chroma syntax highlighting; a .txt file should not. Only check content rows (0 to height-3), not the status bar or message line which always have escapes.
